axis: hexa-runtime / indexed-assign fast path on array_alloc-backed arrays
headline: Indexed-assign fast path collapses 3 host-array ops to 1; alloc+indexed-write now 1.20× FASTER than push (was 2.6× SLOWER per omega_audio_6). Closes audio-6 unblock.

predecessors: omega-interp-3 (cab8ef20): added array_alloc(N) primitive. NULL-RESULT on vocal_hexa_alloc — same speed as push.
omega_audio_6 (just prior): full vocal_hexa migration to alloc+indexed-write was 2.6× SLOWER (155→401ms). Reverted; root cause hypothesized as 'sparse hashmap-style backing'.
context.session_constraint: hexa-only; hexa-lang improvements OK. NO docker/Linux ELF/anima/git changes.

diagnosis.actual_storage_path: CONTIGUOUS, not sparse. The C-side hexa_array_alloc (runtime.c:5717) IS a single malloc(N*sizeof(HexaVal)) + tight loop init — correct, contiguous. The interp-3 dispatch arm in hexa_full.hexa builds via N hexa-side `items.push(zero)` calls which each lower to host hexa_array_push — also tight-grow, not sparse. So the audio-6 'sparse hashmap-style backing' hypothesis was INCORRECT.
diagnosis.actual_root_cause: The IndexAssign hot path did THREE host-array operations per write where push did TWO. Per `arr[i] = v`: (1) get_array(arr) = hexa_array_get(array_store, idx), (2) items[safe_idx] = val = hexa_array_set on items, (3) array_store[arr.int_val] = items = hexa_array_set on array_store (REDUNDANT — `items` is the same pointer that step 2 just mutated in-place; the host hexa_array_set mutates items->arr_ptr->items[i] directly per runtime.c:1190). Plus AssignStmt is NOT @no_arena, so each call paid __hexa_fn_arena_return → hexa_val_heapify scan cost, while array_push_inplace IS @no_arena and skips it.
diagnosis.evidence: Microbench /tmp/_alloc_diag.hexa pre-fix: push 46ms, alloc_idx 72ms (4800 ops, R=5). Net of loop: push ~5.8μs/op, alloc_idx ~11.3μs/op = 1.93× slower per element. Post-fix: alloc_idx 28ms vs push 86ms — REVERSED, alloc_idx now ~3× FASTER per element on the microbench.
diagnosis.speculation_corrected: Original ω-audio-6 root_cause_hypothesis ('sparse/hashmap slot store on alloc-backed arrays') was speculative and wrong. The slowdown was structural in the IndexAssign dispatch path, NOT in alloc's storage backing.

changes.name: array_set_inplace_idx
changes.decorator: @no_arena (suppresses __hexa_fn_arena_enter/return → hexa_val_heapify on every call; mirrors array_push_inplace pattern)
changes.semantics: let items = array_store[arr_val.int_val]; items[idx_int] = val; return val. Mutates items buffer in place via host hexa_array_set; skips redundant array_store write-back (items pointer doesn't move across host hexa_array_set per runtime.c:1190 'In-place mutate (no copy)'). No sole-owner check needed: indexed assign at a logical index never reallocates the slot, never changes refcount.

changes.before: let items = get_array(arr); items[safe_idx] = val; array_store[arr.int_val] = items  (3 host ops + non-no_arena fn frame)
changes.after: array_set_inplace_idx(arr, safe_idx, val)  (1 host op + @no_arena frame)

T4_O_N_scaling_test.interpretation: Perfect linear scaling. 2× N → 2× time (not 4×). Confirms O(N) total for N indexed assigns, NOT O(N²).

vocal_hexa_bench.ab_script: /tmp/_vocal_alloc_ab_bench.hexa (calls vocal_hexa push variant + vocal_hexa_alloc indexed-write variant on same workload)
vocal_hexa_bench.config: 440Hz, gain=0.5, 100ms (n=2400 samples, 4844 byte buffer), R=5 warm runs each

after_fix.verdict: alloc 1.20× FASTER than push — REVERSAL of audio-6 result

byte_hash_check_against_audio_6.interpretation: All 4 organ outputs byte-identical to audio-6. Push variant unchanged. Fix is semantically transparent.

audio_6_full_bench_post_fix.interpretation: push-path unchanged within run-to-run noise; chain modestly improved 499→462ms (likely ambient noise variance, not direct alloc gain since chain doesn't use alloc-indexed yet).

vocal_hexa_bench.note_on_alloc_variant: vocal_hexa_alloc uses raw sin per-sample (no audio-5 period-table opt) so its WAV bytes differ from vocal_hexa output. Both produce valid 4844-byte WAVs. The 137ms alloc time INCLUDES the 4800 sin() calls; if alloc were paired with audio-5 period-table opt, expected to drop further to ~50-70ms range.

net_per_op_post_fix.interpretation: Indexed-write essentially free past loop overhead at this scale — the @no_arena fast path collapses to a single host hexa_array_set.

audio_6_unblock_status.evidence: vocal_hexa A/B post-fix: alloc 137ms vs push 165ms (1.20×). Microbench: alloc_idx 27ms vs push 86ms (3.18×).
next_steps_recommended_for_audio_7_or_la: Re-attempt vocal_hexa migration to alloc+indexed-write path with audio-5 period-table opt PRESERVED. Expected outcome: <70ms target met (per cycle hypothesis).
Migrate breath_hexa, vocal_lf_hexa, pipe_hexa to same pattern — audio-6 deferred these out of caution; root cause now fixed, propagation safe.
Chain bench should drop further once vocal/pipe both on alloc path.
audio_6_unblock_status.deferred_no_more: audio-6 root_cause_hypothesis.next_cycle_suggestion ('FIX is in /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang interpreter') is now LANDED.

build_artifacts.linux_elf_rebuild: NOT performed (out of session scope; macOS-only build)

rollback.risk: LOW. Helper function array_set_inplace_idx is additive. Three call-site rewrites preserve all observable behavior (host hexa_array_set already mutated in-place; the dropped `array_store[idx] = items` was a no-op pointer write per runtime.c:1190 'In-place mutate'). All audio-6 hashes byte-identical post-fix.

constraints_honored.stopped_at: JSON written + vocal_hexa A/B bench captured + selftest PASS

outcome: FIX LANDED + alloc-indexed reverses audio-6 result + selftest PASS + audio-6 unblock CLOSED. The 'sparse hashmap-style backing' diagnosis was wrong; the actual cause was a 3-host-op IndexAssign path vs 2-host-op push path, plus missing @no_arena suppression of fn-frame heapify. One @no_arena helper + 3 call-site simplifications fixed it.
